aboutUs.title: About | St John House Rental
aboutUs.metaDescription: Learn about St. John House Rentals: expert local guides connecting you with ideal island homes & services.
leadParagraphs: If you’re a long-time visitor to St. John, you may remember her from her bartending days at the famous Skinny Legs Bar and Grill.

It was back in those days—in 1999, when the internet first came to Coral Bay—that Mean Jean and her friend Andy Gordon started a website called St. John Links, designed to give intrepid travelers a little info about how to vacation on the sleepy side of the island. Andy was a tech whiz with a neuromuscular disease that caused his fingers to stop working. Mean Jean was, literally, his right hand, moving the computer mouse and doing the typing.

The site started with just a handful of rentals and the idea was simple: connect vacationers directly with property owners looking to rent out their homes. No fuss. No fees. No middleman. If you saw a house you liked, you could contact the homeowner directly and book. The homeowner would give a percentage to St. John Links. And—in the true Coral Bay fashion—it was all done on the honor system.

People liked this idea. The business grew. In 2004 St. John Links added St. John House Rentals to accommodate the demand. Today, the site is going strong, giving you an easy way to connect directly with dozens of St. John property owners.

If you’re looking for the best way to support local, you’ve found it. As for Mean Jean, she’s no longer behind the bar at Skinny’s but she’s still on the island, still exploring all the hidden beauties of her home, and still doing one of those things that makes St. John such a magical place: making connections.

is essential to making your stay unforgettable. Whether you’re visiting for a week or planning an extended stay, we have a home for you. From cozy cottages to spacious villas, we offer properties in some of the most desirable locations on St. John, giving you access to pristine beaches, hiking trails, and breathtaking views.

aboutUs.contentHtml:
Celebrating 25 years this season!

ABOUT US

St. John House Rentals is owned and run by Jean "Mean Jean" Vance

If you’re a long-time visitor to St. John, you may remember her from her bartending days at the famous Skinny Legs Bar and Grill.


It was back in those days—in 1999, when the internet first came to Coral Bay—that Mean Jean and her friend Andy Gordon started a website called St. John Links, designed to give intrepid travelers a little info about how to vacation on the sleepy side of the island. Andy was a tech whiz with a neuromuscular disease that caused his fingers to stop working. Mean Jean was, literally, his right hand, moving the computer mouse and doing the typing.

The site started with just a handful of rentals and the idea was simple: connect vacationers directly with property owners looking to rent out their homes. No fuss. No fees. No middleman. If you saw a house you liked, you could contact the homeowner directly and book. The homeowner would give a percentage to St. John Links. And—in the true Coral Bay fashion—it was all done on the honor system.


People liked this idea. The business grew. In 2004 St. John Links added St. John House Rentals to accommodate the demand. Today, the site is going strong, giving you an easy way to connect directly with dozens of St. John property owners.


If you’re looking for the best way to support local, you’ve found it. As for Mean Jean, she’s no longer behind the bar at Skinny’s but she’s still on the island, still exploring all the hidden beauties of her home, and still doing one of those things that makes St. John such a magical place: making connections.

ESSENTIALS

We believe that finding the right home

is essential to making your stay unforgettable. Whether you’re visiting for a week or planning an extended stay, we have a home for you. From cozy cottages to spacious villas, we offer properties in some of the most desirable locations on St. John, giving you access to pristine beaches, hiking trails, and breathtaking views.

propertyForSale.title: Property For Sale | St John House Rental
leadParagraphs: If you’ve ever vacationed in an exotic location and wished you could stay forever, you’re not alone. Although it might seem unattainable, owning a piece of the rock can be your reality.

St. John is rich with history and culture. One of the most attractive aspects of St. John is the Virgin Islands National Park, which Laurance Rockefeller helped to establish in 1956. Since then, the VINP on St. John has grown to 7,200 acres plus 5,600 underwater acres. This equates to approximately 80 percent of the island remaining untouched, including pristine white sand beaches.

Visitors are attracted to this small island from all walks of life and from every part of the globe. Visitors and residents alike enjoy hiking trails, swimming, surfing, snorkeling, scuba diving, and sailing, along with an array of other outdoor activities.

Regardless of your budget, the brokers and sales agents of 340 Real Estate Co. can show and sell all the properties that are on the MLS whether it be Commercial properties, Residential homes, Land, or Condos. We have an up to date comprehensive online database of all the properties for sale on the St John Board of Realtors MLS as well as a database of St John Real Estate Sales History, including MLS Homes, Land, and Condos sold on St John since 2009!
Whether it be for a week or a lifetime, contact us today and let us put our collective 50-plus years of real estate experience to work, helping you realize your dream of owning a piece of St John.”
Tammy Donnelly VI Licensed Real Estate Broker /Owner www.340realestateco.com Phone: 340-643-6068

Get your ticket and drive into the large parking lot. An employee will guide you to your parking spot. You will wait in this spot until it is your turn to load onto the barge.

**Note that you will back your vehicle onto the barge. 
 

One-way or round-trip tickets are purchased after you are on the barge. Tickets are not interchangeable between the three car barge companies. If you purchase a round-trip ticket, it is only good for the company that issued it.
 
The Red Hook ferry location services both passenger ferries and car barges at separate well-marked entrances. Arrive as early as possible before the car barge departure time you hope to catch. It fills up fast.

Three companies currently offer daily service between Enighed Pond, St. John and Red Hook, St. Thomas for vehicles. A car ferry, or car barge, takes cars between St. Thomas and St. John! The driver and passengers ride along with the vehicle too.

Car Barge Information

First thing to know about is the Port Authority Fee
Pay at the booth before you enter the parking lot at Red Hook
$3 per Car, small SUV, Motorcycle; $4 per truck, van, large SUV.

Get your ticket and drive in, there will be someone to show you which lane to get in. Note that you will back your vehicle on to the ferry.

The fare for the car barge is paid once on the actual barge. Tickets can be purchased for a one-way trip, or a round-trip fare. Tickets are not interchangeable between the three car barge companies. If you purchase a round-trip ticket, it is only good for the barge that issued it.

The Red Hook ferry location services both passenger ferries and car ferries, but there are two separate docking areas for each type of ferry, with separate well-marked entrances. Arrive as early as possible before the car barge departure time you hope to catch. It fills up fast.

VI Now has more information Link for information is https://www.vinow.com/stjohn/getting_there/car-ferry-st-thomas-and-st-john/

charterBoats.metaDescription: Rent sailboat & powerboat charters in St. John’s crystal waters. Snorkel tours & day sails await!
leadParagraphs: Rent a charter boat for an unforgettable St. John adventure.

Would you like a guided snorkel tour? St. John in the US Virgin Islands boasts some of the most beautiful waters and beaches in the world. Connections East offers a range of charter options, including sailboat and powerboat rentals, to help make your St. John experience truly unforgettable. Whether you want a relaxing sail along the coast or an adventurous snorkeling excursion, our charters provide the perfect way to explore the stunning beauty of the Virgin Islands.

The Virgin Islands Territorial Emergency Management Agency (VITEMA) is an excellent resource for disaster preparedness and response. Hurricane season is June 1st through November 30th. You can find basic information, tracking maps, emergency communication methods and kit suggestions for the various dangerous conditions you can encounter in the Caribbean islands. Visit them at https://vitema.vi.gov/
The USCG has provided the following link for those who are interested in learning more. https://www.rentalboatsafety.com/

Understanding the Fair Information Practice Principles and how they should be implemented is critical to comply with the various privacy laws that protect personal information. In order to be in line with Fair Information Practices we will take the following responsive action, should a data breach occur: We will notify you via email • Within 7 business days We will notify the users via in-site notification • Within 7 business days We also agree to the Individual Redress Principle which requires that individuals have the right to legally pursue enforceable rights against data collectors and processors who fail to adhere to the law. links.metaDescription: Essential St. John links: local services, tourism info, transportation & tips for your perfect stay.
leadParagraphs: to St. John or just taking a quick virtual break during a busy day, thanks for visiting us!

St John Links helps you plan your special Caribbean vacation to our beautiful island and provides all the essential information you need while you're here! Returning to paradise? We’ve got tips on things to do, where to stay, and "things you need to know," like how to get around and where to find the everyday services you need while visiting paradise. First-time visitor? Start here!

Our information page includes tips on island manners, important phone numbers, Western Union services, and much more. With picture-perfect weather, a diverse community, and beautiful places of worship, you’ll feel right at home. We’ve also included medical info such as hospitals, clinics, and other emergency numbers, just in case. US citizens don’t need a passport to visit – we’re part of the US! However, we’ve also got passport information if needed. If you’re staying for a while and hope to volunteer, you can help our seniors or join the volunteers keeping our National Park pristine. Make a difference!

Looking for a St. John Virgin Island rental home, vacation villa, or intimate Caribbean cottage in Cruz Bay or Coral Bay? How about a relaxing day sail or an exciting sports fishing adventure? St John Links has it all – boat rentals, charter boats, snorkeling, world-class sailing, fishing, and horseback riding. And when you need a break from vacationing, we have some of the best massage therapists around! When planning your vacation on the beautiful island of St. John, consider everything that can make your trip even more memorable. The St John Links website helps you find the right accommodation, transportation, and everyday services. St. John is the most natural and beautiful US Virgin Island, home to world-class beaches, rich tropical foliage, and endemic birds. Check out books written by local authors, and use our map and ferry information to help you navigate around. Your trip may include hiking trails, snorkeling tours, or simply exploring in your rental car – it’s your vacation!
St. John restaurants offer everything from fine dining to casual meals. Discover more about our paradise today!
